title: ‘Thor: Love and Thunder’ and Five Ways to Fix the Marvel Crisis
format: Charles Holmes and Rob Mahoney join Sean to autopsy Thor: Love and Thunder, rank all of Phase Four, and pitch ways out of the Marvel malaise: an X-Men/Spider-Man crossover, Avengers vs. X-Men, and a Denis Villeneuve-directed Silver Surfer movie.

segments.heading: Thor: Love and Thunder
segments.note: Sean Fennessy, Charles Holmes, and Rob Mahoney react

films.title: Thor: Love and Thunder
films.note: Biggest opening weekend of any Thor movie, but the second-lowest CinemaScore in MCU history. Christian Bale's Gorr the God Butcher is the consensus best part; everyone else is lukewarm at best.

segments.heading: Is everyone just exhausted by Marvel?
segments.note: Box office, CinemaScores, and the case for franchise fatigue

films.title: Thor: Ragnarok
films.note: The standard Love and Thunder keeps getting measured against, unfavorably. It's light on its feet where the new one feels effortful.

films.title: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
films.note: One of three of the four lowest-CinemaScore MCU movies to land in the last seven months.

films.title: Eternals
films.note: Called the low point of Phase Four: the one real formal swing, and a total miss.

films.title: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings
films.note: Charles says his patience with the MCU was already gone by this one.

films.title: Black Widow
films.note: The pandemic-delayed one they gave a pass at the time.

films.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
films.note: Consensus best of Phase Four as an experience, though both hosts admit it doesn't hold together as a movie.

films.title: The Avengers
films.note: The sixth film of Phase One, and the bar for how big a 'sixth film' is supposed to be.

films.title: Iron Man
films.note: Cited for how much room Downey was given to just riff, something they say wouldn't fly in a movie today.

films.title: Ant-Man
films.note: Sixth film of Phase Two, their example of the harmless, good-enough Marvel movie they say doesn't get made anymore.

films.title: Captain America: The Winter Soldier
films.note: What Ant-Man led directly into; the high-water mark they're measuring current Marvel against.

films.title: Black Panther
films.note: Sixth film of Phase Three.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: Supposed to clear the decks and set a clean foundation; Rob argues Marvel never followed through on that.

films.title: Thor
films.note: Neither host will rewatch it. Charles: 'the bleached eyebrows in that movie are insane.'

films.title: Uncharted
films.note: The one non-Marvel movie a Marvel lead (Tom Holland) has opened lately, and it wasn't good.

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3
films.note: Not yet out at recording time; Sean says he'd have been shocked, at the first Guardians' release, to hear there'd eventually be four of these.

films.title: Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania
films.note: The then-unreleased third Ant-Man, name-checked as one more sequel nobody was asking for.

segments.heading: Ranking Phase Four
segments.note: best to worst, per Charles and Rob
segments.subject: MCU Phase Four

films.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
films.note: No. 1 for both.

films.title: Thor: Love and Thunder
films.note: No. 2 for both, which Rob says more about how weak this batch is than anything else.

films.title: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
films.note: No. 3 for both.

films.title: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings
films.note: No. 4 for both. Rob shouts out Tony Leung.

films.title: Eternals
films.note: No. 5 for Charles, dead last for Rob, who says he watched it with open contempt.

films.title: Black Widow
films.note: No. 6 for Charles (who says the bottom three are interchangeable), No. 5 for Rob.

segments.heading: Life outside the MCU, and the Taika backlash
segments.note: What's actually stealing the pop-culture conversation from Marvel this summer

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: The movie people want to talk about instead of Doctor Strange, a surprise even to them.

films.title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
films.note: Named the actual best multiversal movie of the year.

films.title: RRR
films.note: Named the year's best action, over anything Marvel has put out.

films.title: Free Guy
films.note: Taika Waititi cameo No. 1 in the overexposure pile-on.

films.title: The Suicide Squad
films.note: Taika cameo No. 2.

films.title: Lightyear
films.note: Taika voice role No. 3, around where Charles says the internet started to turn on him.

films.title: What We Do in the Shadows
films.note: Charles's litmus test for Taika: a movie for a specific audience, not a mass one, which is what made him such an odd MCU pick to begin with.

segments.heading: Should Marvel just hit reset?
segments.note: Full reboot vs. quietly retiring characters

films.title: Captain Marvel
films.note: Cited as proof the 'quiet retirement' approach is already happening: a sequel that keeps getting pushed back.

segments.heading: Fix No. 1: the X-Men/Spider-Man crossover Marvel should already be building
segments.note: Rob pitches Brad Bird directing an X-Men team built to meet Spider-Man; Charles counters that the solo movie is dead and pitches a teen-movie pipeline funneling into Avengers vs. X-Men ahead of Secret Wars

films.title: The Incredibles
films.note: The tone Rob wants Brad Bird to bring to X-Men.

films.title: Mission: Impossible – Ghost Protocol
films.note: The action sensibility Rob wants from a Brad Bird X-Men.

films.title: West Side Story
films.note: Rob's case for Rachel Zegler, recast mid-pitch from Jean Grey to Kitty Pryde.

films.title: Captain America: Civil War
films.note: The legislated-superpowers story model Rob explicitly doesn't want to redo to fold the X-Men in.

films.title: The Guest
films.note: Rob's pitch for Dan Stevens as Wolverine rests on the menace this movie shows he can play.

segments.heading: Fix No. 2: Sean's Silver Surfer movie
segments.note: Denis Villeneuve directs Keanu Reeves as the Silver Surfer and Michael Shannon as Galactus, tied to a Fantastic Four led by Adam Driver, Elle Fanning, Jesse Plemons, and Dev Patel

films.title: Hustle
films.note: Sean's reference point for the Silver Surfer's arc: a reluctant company man having a crisis of faith.

films.title: Dune: Part Two
films.note: Why Sean thinks Villeneuve is wrong for the MCU on paper but right in practice: he already made a corny, unadaptable story feel essential.

films.title: Marriage Story
films.note: The joke pitch for what an all-Adam-Driver Fantastic Four would actually look like.

films.title: Not Another Teen Movie
films.note: What Sean says Chris Evans actually ported into his Human Torch performance.

films.title: The Green Knight
films.note: Sean's case for Dev Patel as Johnny Storm: the vulnerability under the bravado.

films.title: Fantastic Four: Rise of the Silver Surfer
films.note: The one prior big-screen Silver Surfer, which both hosts agree was botched.

Ads: The Ringer Reality TV Show (Challenge USA cold open)
